heading: Char Dham Yatra Itinerary – 9 Nights 10 Days (Trusted Pilgrimage Package)
intro: Plan your sacred Char Dham Yatra with a well-paced, safety-first itinerary covering Yamunotri Temple, Gangotri Temple, Kedarnath Temple, and Badrinath Temple. This 9 Nights / 10 Days Char Dham Yatra itinerary is designed for comfortable travel, proper acclimatisation, and smooth darshan, making it ideal for families, senior citizens, and first-time pilgrims.

overview.subHeading: Overview of Char Dham Yatra Package
overview.content: The Char Dham Yatra is one of the most sacred Hindu pilgrimages in India, undertaken through the Himalayan regions of Uttarakhand. Our itinerary follows the traditional route sequence and avoids rushed travel or unsafe same-day long drives.

highlights.title: Package Highlights
items: Covers all four Char Dhams in correct order
Balanced travel pace with sufficient rest

days.title: Day 01: Haridwar / Dehradun – Rishikesh – Barkot
days.content: Arrival at Haridwar or Dehradun. Proceed to Rishikesh for Char Dham Yatra registration and vehicle Green Card. After completing government formalities, drive to Barkot (approx. 216 km). Note: Green Card verification may take 4–5 hours as per government procedure. Overnight stay at Barkot.

days.title: Day 02: Barkot – Yamunotri – Barkot
days.content: Early morning drive to Janakichatti / Phoolchatti. Start the trek to Yamunotri Temple (approx. 6 km one way). Options available: Walking, Pony / Doli (at own cost). At Yamunotri: Holy dip at Jamunabai Kund, Darshan & Pooja of Goddess Yamuna. Return to Barkot by evening. Overnight stay at Barkot.

days.title: Day 03: Barkot – Uttarkashi
days.content: Morning free till lunch. Post-lunch drive to Uttarkashi (approx. 80 km). Hotel check-in on arrival. Evening visit to Kashi Vishwanath Temple, Uttarkashi, located on the banks of River Ganga. Overnight stay at Uttarkashi.

days.title: Day 04: Uttarkashi – Gangotri – Uttarkashi
days.content: Early morning departure (around 4:00 AM) to Gangotri (approx. 100 km / 5 hours). En-route: Holy dip at Gangnani hot water springs, Scenic drive through Harsil Valley. At Gangotri: Holy dip in River Bhagirathi, Temple darshan and pooja. Return to Uttarkashi by evening. Overnight stay at Uttarkashi.

days.title: Day 05: Uttarkashi – Guptkashi
days.content: After breakfast, drive to Guptkashi (approx. 230 km). En-route view the Mandakini River at Tilwara. On arrival: Hotel check-in, Visit Ardh Narishwar Temple. Overnight stay at Guptkashi.

days.title: Day 06: Guptkashi – Kedarnath
days.content: Early morning drive to Sonprayag, then take a government jeep to Gaurikund. From Gaurikund, begin the trek to Kedarnath (approx. 17 km one way). Travel options: Trekking, Pony / Doli, Helicopter (from Phata / Sirsi / Guptkashi helipad). After darshan and pooja, overnight stay at Kedarnath Ji.

days.title: Day 07: Kedarnath – Guptkashi
days.content: Early morning darshan (optional). Trek down to Gaurikund and return to Guptkashi via Sonprayag. Hotel check-in on arrival. Overnight stay at Guptkashi.

days.title: Day 08: Guptkashi – Pipalkoti
days.content: After breakfast, drive to Pipalkoti. En-route witness famous Prayag (river confluences) of Uttarakhand. Evening free for rest and acclimatization. Overnight stay at Pipalkoti.

days.title: Day 09: Pipalkoti – Badrinath – Pipalkoti
days.content: After breakfast, drive to Badrinath (approx. 80 km). At Badrinath: Holy bath at Tapt Kund, Darshan of Badrivishal, Visit Brahma Kapal (ancestral rituals). Optional sightseeing (time permitting): Mana Village, Vyas Gufa, Saraswati River origin. Return to Pipalkoti. Overnight stay at Pipalkoti.

days.title: Day 10: Pipalkoti – Haridwar / Dehradun
days.content: After breakfast, drive back to Haridwar or Dehradun. Drop off at the railway station/airport for onward journey.

reasons.subHeading: Why Choose Our Char Dham Yatra Package

items.title: Experience-Driven Planning
items.description: Our itineraries are built from real on-ground Char Dham operations, not copied templates.

items.title: Correct Itinerary Flow
items.description: We avoid unsafe same-day return plans and unrealistic travel timings.

items.title: Honest & Transparent Guidance
items.description: No "guaranteed darshan" or fake helicopter promises. We explain real conditions clearly.

items.title: Local Network Support
items.description: Coordination with local drivers, hotels, and ground staff across Uttarakhand.

trustCred.subtitle: Hands-on experience managing Char Dham yatras
bullets: Safety-first approach in weather or road disruptions
trustCred.closing: We prioritise pilgrim safety and spiritual experience over rushed schedules.

sections.title: Altitude sickness: symptoms & prevention
sections.content: Layman's checklist: acclimatise, hydrate, avoid alcohol, know symptoms, carry Diamox only on doctor advice.

sections.title: Medical facilities & emergency contacts
sections.content: Suggest nearest hospitals & mountain rescue numbers; recommend travel insurance & ambulance/air evacuation info.

sections.title: Packing checklist (downloadable)
sections.content: Clothes (layers), footwear, medicines, first-aid, power bank, copies of documents, rain gear, trekking poles, torch, sunscreen.

items.title: Types of stays (hotels, guesthouses, ashrams, dharamshalas)
items.content: Quick notes on booking: book early in peak season (May, autumn), what to expect at high altitudes.

items.title: Sample hotel categories (budget / mid / premium) and approximate price ranges
items.content: Provide practical advice: carry cash, limited card POS in remote areas.

items.title: Food & water safety
items.content: Tips on what's safe to eat; bottled water, boiled water, carry ORS.

weather.bestTime: Late April → mid-June (post-opening) and September → October (post-monsoon, crisp weather and clear skies). Monsoon (July–August) brings landslides and road closures — plan around it.
weather.title: Weather snapshot (by month):

months.description: melting snow, cold mornings, daytime pleasant at lower altitudes.
ideal window, stable weather on most days.
heavy rainfall risk; not recommended for road travel.
excellent visibility and autumn colours; roads re-open after monsoon.

importantNotes.content: The final Char Dham Yatra itinerary will be shared after booking confirmation. The detailed day-wise plan may vary based on the package selected, hotel category, travel mode (road / helicopter), weather conditions, and government regulations prevailing at the time of travel. We ensure that the finalized itinerary is practical, safe, and aligned with real-time ground conditions, keeping pilgrim comfort and safety as the top priority.
